target.title: test(config): keep agent model overrides selection-only
target.body:
## Summary
- keep `agents.list[].model` on the same selection-only schema as other agent model overrides
- keep `agents.list[].subagents.model` selection-only as well
- add regression coverage so `timeoutMs` stays limited to the tool/media model config surfaces that actually consume it

Follow-up to #83339. Supersedes the original timeout-support direction for #83291.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: agent and subagent model override config should not silently accept `timeoutMs`; timeout config belongs on the tool/media model schemas that consume it.
- Real environment tested: Windows OpenClaw worktree rebased on `origin/main` at `cb408bb06b`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/schema.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  43 passed (43)
```

- Observed result after fix: the schema test passes while rejecting `agents.list[0].model.timeoutMs` and `agents.list[0].subagents.model.timeoutMs`.
- What was not tested: live agent runtime execution, because this PR only locks the config schema contract.

## Root Cause
The original PR direction treated model selection objects like tool model config objects. Current main now separates those contracts: model selection chooses a model (`primary`/`fallbacks`), while timeout values belong to surfaces that own timeout behavior.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level: focused config schema unit test.
- Target file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: per-agent model and subagent model overrides reject `timeoutMs`.

## Validation
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/schema.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: Improve Telegram groups config shape diagnostics
target.body:
Fixes #83083.

## Summary

- Add Telegram-owned doctor diagnostics when `channels.telegram.groups` or account-level Telegram `groups` is an array, string, `null`, or another non-object shape.
- Keep core raw channel config validation plugin-agnostic: it still reports the failing path and schema error, but does not mention Telegram-specific repairs.
- Keep the schema strict: this only improves diagnostics and does not auto-migrate arbitrary non-object values.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Malformed Telegram group config such as `channels.telegram.groups: ["-1001234567890"]` failed with only a generic raw config validation error, and doctor did not explain the expected Telegram group/topic object-map shape.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows, head `357a224`, running focused config validation and Telegram doctor tests against malformed Telegram groups config shapes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
  src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts \
  --reporter=dot

git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** Focused tests passed:

```text
src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts: 8 tests passed
extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts: 18 tests passed
```

The config validation test now asserts the generic raw validation output stays plugin-agnostic and does not contain Telegram-specific doctor guidance. The Telegram doctor test asserts malformed Telegram groups shapes produce the Telegram-owned warning, including the expected object-map shape.

**Observed result after fix:** Core config validation remains generic for invalid raw channel config, while Telegram doctor preview warnings surface the malformed groups shape in the plugin boundary.

**What was not tested:** No live Telegram bot or forum topic was contacted; this is a config/doctor diagnostic-only change and intentionally does not send Telegram API calls or migrate arbitrary invalid values.

## Verification

- `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts --reporter=dot` — passed, 26 tests.
- `git diff --check` — passed.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Converts the next interactive doctor maintenance bucket to the structured `detect()` / `repair()` health contract introduced by #83198:

- shell completion detection and cached-completion repair
- startup channel maintenance as a repair-only structured health check
- Linux systemd linger detection and guided repair for local Gateway services

The legacy doctor contribution entries stay in place for ordering/backward compatibility, but their repair paths now defer to the structured checks during `doctor --fix` so the work is not duplicated. The converted contributions also advertise their `healthCheckIds` so the conversion inventory and filtering metadata stay aligned.

## Behavior

- `doctor --lint` can report shell-completion and systemd-linger findings without mutating state.
- `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion` stays quiet when shell completion is simply not installed, because completion install is optional.
- `doctor --fix` runs the corresponding `repair()` path, then the doctor repair driver can validate with the same scoped `detect()` logic.
- Startup channel maintenance remains repair-only: it is intentionally quiet in lint mode, but participates in the structured repair pipeline during `doctor --fix`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Follow-up doctor maintenance checks migrate from legacy `run(ctx)` repair logic into structured health checks without changing the user-facing repair path; optional shell completion installs do not become lint failures.
- **Real environment tested**: WSL Ubuntu checkout cloned from `giodl73-repo/openclaw:doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance` after the latest push.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json

node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
  src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts \
  src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Earlier focused conversion proof on this branch also ran:

```bash
CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 \
  pnpm test \
    src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts \
    src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts \
    src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts \
    src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts \
    src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts \
    src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts \
    -- --reporter=dot

node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix**: The real CLI lint proof returned:

```json
{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":19,"findings":[]}
```

The focused regression tests passed after the P1 fix:

```text
src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts: 7 tests passed
src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts: 5 tests passed
```

Earlier conversion proof passed 3 Vitest shards: 6 test files, 26 tests. Both core and test `tsgo` checks completed with no output. `oxfmt --check` reported all matched files use the correct format. `git diff --check` passed.

- **Observed result after fix**: Shell completion, startup channel maintenance, and systemd linger conversions are covered by focused tests. The real `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json` command runs the converted health check and remains clean when completion is optional/missing.
- **What was not tested**: No live systemd service was modified. The systemd linger repair path is covered with injected `loginctl` dependencies rather than changing the host login manager. I did not run a live shell-profile `doctor --fix` install because that would modify the proof host profile; the cache/install repair behavior is covered by injected repair tests.

## Verification

target.title: fix(update): guide EACCES manual recovery
target.body:
﻿## Summary

- add EACCES recovery hints that tell managed-Gateway operators to stop the Gateway before sudo/manual package recovery
- document the root-owned Linux system-global recovery path: stop Gateway, run the system npm install, refresh the Gateway service, restart, then verify
- add focused coverage for staged/global install EACCES hint text

## Why

Current main can restart the old managed Gateway after a staged package update fails with EACCES. The existing recovery hint points operators toward sudo/manual package recovery, but it does not say to stop the Gateway first. That leaves a window where the running Gateway can try to load core/plugin files while npm is replacing the package tree.

This follows ClawSweeper's narrow guidance on #83747: improve recovery hints/docs and focused tests without changing the updater lifecycle policy.

Closes #83747

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** EACCES recovery guidance for root-owned/system-global npm installs now fails closed at the operator level by instructing users to stop the managed Gateway before manual package replacement.
- **Real environment tested:** Ubuntu VPS disposable npm-global proof environment, Node `v22.22.0`, npm `10.9.4`, throwaway `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=proof83757`, throwaway `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX` under `/tmp`. Also validated in a Windows desktop source checkout from upstream main `424c6d0a5`, Node `v24.13.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Installed `openclaw@latest` into a temp npm prefix on the VPS, applied this PR's EACCES hint change to the packaged update CLI bundle in that disposable install, made only the temp prefix's `lib/node_modules` unwritable to trigger the real `global install stage` EACCES path, then ran `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=proof83757 NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<temp-prefix> openclaw update --no-restart --yes --tag latest --timeout 20`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the disposable VPS OpenClaw setup:

```text
ENV: VPS disposable proof, node=v22.22.0 npm=10.9.4 profile=proof83757
PATCH: disposable install now contains PR #83757 EACCES hint text
OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (50a2481)
COMMAND: OPENCLAW_PROFILE=proof83757 NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<temp-prefix> openclaw update --no-restart --yes --tag latest --timeout 20
EXIT_CODE: 1

Update Result: ERROR
  Root: /tmp/openclaw-83757-proof-REDACTED/prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  Reason: global install stage
  Before: 2026.5.18
  After: 2026.5.18

Steps:
  x global install stage (0ms)
      EACCES: permission denied, mkdtemp '/tmp/openclaw-83757-proof-REDACTED/prefix/lib/node_modules/.openclaw-update-stage-S2S8Ob'

Recovery hints:
  - Detected permission failure (EACCES). Re-run with a writable global prefix or sudo (for system-managed Node installs).
  - If you recover with sudo/manual package install on a managed Gateway, stop the Gateway first so it does not load files while the package tree is being replaced.
  - Example: npm config set prefix ~/.local && npm i -g openclaw@latest
  - System install outline: openclaw gateway stop -> sudo <system-npm> i -g openclaw@latest -> openclaw gateway install --force -> openclaw gateway restart.

Total time: 473ms
ASSERT: proof output contains EACCES recovery hints from patched CLI/update path
CLEANUP: removed disposable proof directory
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real CLI/update `global install stage` EACCES path prints the new stop-before-manual-recovery guidance and the system install outline with `gateway install --force` and `gateway restart`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not perform a destructive package replacement against the live production OpenClaw install; this PR intentionally changes guidance/tests only and does not alter package-manager lifecycle behavior. The VPS proof used a disposable temp npm prefix/cache/home/profile only. The temp proof directory was removed after capture. I did not hotfix, cherry-pick into, stop, restart, or otherwise mutate the live VPS OpenClaw install/gateway.

## Validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       6 passed (6)
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output
```

```text
codex-review
codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```

target.title: fix(exec): block configured denied path operands
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: issue #11829 calls out agent access to provider/API key material as a security roadmap gap, and the issue discussion proposed a configurable `tools.exec.deniedPaths` surface for tenant-controlled secret paths.
- Solution: add `tools.exec.deniedPaths` as an exec preflight guard that rejects commands referencing configured paths such as `/run/secrets/**` before gateway/node/sandbox execution. The guard now inspects command-carrier and shell-wrapper payloads, sandbox checks resolve relative operands in the container path namespace, node-host checks run after node target resolution using the target node platform namespace, config loading preserves authored denied-path `~` patterns until execution, home-relative denied patterns such as `~/.openclaw/credentials/**` resolve against the selected exec HOME, and `$HOME` / `${HOME}` command operands are expanded before denied-path matching.
- What changed: runtime guard, global/agent config wiring, zod/type/schema help/labels/tags, docs, generated config baseline, config path normalization, and regression tests for direct paths, shell-wrapper payloads, command substitutions, sandbox container-relative traversal, node-host Windows path semantics, home-relative denied patterns, and HOME-prefixed command operands.
- What did NOT change: no changes to model catalog filtering, auth proxy docs, or the existing models.json key-stripping work already covered by other PRs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #11829
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- This gives operators a config-level guard for mounted secret directories, especially deployments that keep provider credentials in predictable paths such as `/run/secrets/**`. It is defense-in-depth rather than a replacement for sandboxing or secret isolation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: exec commands can currently reference mounted sensitive paths unless deployment isolation catches the access; after this patch a configured denied path is rejected before host execution.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node 22.22.0, invoking the real `createExecTool` runtime with `host: "gateway"`, `security: "full"`, `ask: "off"`, and `deniedPaths: ["/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/**"]`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` importing `./src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`, creating the exec tool, and calling `tool.execute(...)` for these commands:
  - `cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"`
  - `bash -lc 'cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"'`
  - `bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key")"'`
- Evidence after fix (copied live local runtime output):

```text
blocked: cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"
Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
blocked: bash -lc 'cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key"'
Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
blocked: bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key")"'
Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
```

- Observed result after fix: the exec tool throws before running each command, including the shell-wrapper and command-substitution forms, so the denied path is not handed to gateway execution.
- Additional sandbox namespace proof: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` now covers `host: "sandbox"`, `workdir: "/workspace"`, `deniedPaths: ["/run/secrets/**"]`, and `cat ../../run/secrets/provider.key`. The preflight resolves the operand against `/workspace` using POSIX container semantics and rejects `/run/secrets/provider.key` before `buildExecSpec` is called.
- Additional node namespace proof: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts` now covers `host: "node"` after a Windows node target resolves with `platform: "win32"`, `workdir: "C:\\Work"`, `deniedPaths: ["C:\\Secrets\\**"]`, and `type C:\\Secrets\\provider.key`. The node path preflight rejects the command before `system.run` is called. It also covers case-insensitive Windows matching with `type c:\\secrets\\provider.key`.
- Additional home-relative proof: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` covers `deniedPaths: ["~/.openclaw/credentials/**"]` and blocks commands referencing the resolved HOME credential path via literal resolved paths, `$HOME/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key`, and a shell-wrapper `${HOME}/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` payload. `src/config/normalize-paths.test.ts` verifies authored `tools.exec.deniedPaths` entries stay home-relative during generic config path normalization so the exec guard can resolve them later in the selected runtime namespace.
- Additional config-through-runtime namespace proof: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` flows `normalizeConfigPaths({ tools: { exec: { deniedPaths: ["~/.openclaw/credentials/**"] } } })` into sandbox exec with `HOME=/workspace` and proves `$HOME/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` is blocked as `/workspace/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key`. `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts` flows the same normalized config into a Windows node target with `HOME=C:\\Users\\agent` and proves `C:\\Users\\agent\\.openclaw\\credentials\\provider.key` is blocked before `system.run`.
- What was not tested: a full multi-tenant Docker deployment with real mounted provider secrets.
- Before evidence: the new regression tests failed before the follow-up fixes because `bash -lc 'cat "<deniedFile>"'`, `bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "<deniedFile>")"'`, the sandbox container-relative traversal, the Windows node target command, the home-relative denied pattern, and `$HOME` / `${HOME}` command operands all resolved successfully with `(no output)` or left path prefixes unexpanded instead of rejecting.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: exec had approval/safe-bin/env guards, but no config-level path denylist for sensitive operand paths. The first implementation scanned only the outer argv tokens, used host path semantics for sandbox relative operands, checked node targets before the target platform was known, let generic config normalization consume authored home-relative denied patterns before runtime namespace selection, did not normalize home-relative denied patterns, and did not expand common `$HOME` / `${HOME}` command operands before matching.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test asserted that a configured secret path is blocked inside a shell wrapper, command substitution, sandbox container-relative path, Windows node target path, `~/...` denied pattern, or HOME-prefixed command operand before execution.
- Contributing context (if known): issue #11829 covers a broader roadmap; this PR implements only the `tools.exec.deniedPaths` part.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`
- Scenarios the tests lock in:
  - direct `cat "<deniedFile>"` is rejected;
  - `bash -lc 'cat "<deniedFile>"'` is rejected;
  - `bash -lc 'echo "$(cat "<deniedFile>")"'` is rejected;
  - sandbox `cat ../../run/secrets/provider.key` from `/workspace` is rejected as `/run/secrets/provider.key` before `buildExecSpec`;
  - node-host `type C:\Secrets\provider.key` is rejected after resolving the target node as `platform: "win32"` and before `system.run`;
  - node-host Windows denied-path matching is case-insensitive for `C:\Secrets\**` versus `c:\secrets\provider.key`;
  - `~/.openclaw/credentials/**` is runtime-resolved and blocks the selected HOME credential path;
  - config-loaded `~/.openclaw/credentials/**` stays authored through generic path normalization and blocks sandbox `HOME=/workspace`;
  - config-loaded `~/.openclaw/credentials/**` stays authored through generic path normalization and blocks Windows node `HOME=C:\Users\agent`;
  - `$HOME/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` and shell-wrapper `${HOME}/.openclaw/credentials/provider.key` operands are expanded before matching and rejected.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises `createExecTool` directly and proves the preflight guard runs even when approvals are bypassed.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional config key: `tools.exec.deniedPaths`. Commands that reference matching paths now fail before exec starts.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
exec command -> env/path setup -> host execution may receive /run/secrets/provider.key

After:
exec command -> env/path setup -> denied path preflight -> Security Violation before host execution
```

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/config/normalize-paths.test.ts` -> 119 passed
- `pnpm config:schema:check` -> ok
- `pnpm config:docs:check` -> ok
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> ok
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/config/normalize-paths.ts src/config/normalize-paths.test.ts src/config/io.ts` -> all matched files use correct format
- `git diff --check` -> ok

target.title: fix: resolve response prefix template variables in abort replies
target.body:
## Summary

Template variables are not populated when user issues early stop commands. 

- **Problem:** When a user sends a "stop" command (e.g. on WhatsApp), the abort reply displays unresolved template variables: `{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`
- **Why it matters:** The raw `{model}` and `{thinkingLevel}` placeholders leak into user-facing messages, looking broken and confusing.
- **What changed:** `createReplyPrefixContext()` now pre-seeds the `ResponsePrefixContext` with model, provider, and thinkingLevel resolved from config defaults, so early-exit paths like abort have valid values for template interpolation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to abort logic, dispatch flow, template resolution engine, or `formatAbortReplyText()`. The `onModelSelected()` callback still overwrites the defaults at runtime during normal replies.

## Change Type (select all)

- [X] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [X] Gateway / orchestration
- [X] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #45314

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Abort replies now display the resolved model name and thinking level from config (e.g. `kimi-k2.5 - off 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`) instead of literal `{model} - {thinkingLevel}` placeholders.
- Applies to all channels using a `responsePrefix` template with `{model}` or `{thinkingLevel}` variables.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / pnpm
- Model/provider: openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5
- Integration/channel: WhatsApp (reproduces on any channel with `responsePrefix` template)
- Relevant config:
  ```json
  {
    "messages": {
      "responsePrefix": "{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: "
    }
  }
  ```

### Steps

1. Configure a `responsePrefix` with `{model}` and/or `{thinkingLevel}` template variables
2. Start a conversation so the agent is processing
3. Send "stop" or `/stop`

### Expected

- Reply: `kimi-k2.5 - off 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`

### Actual

- Reply: `{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`

## Evidence
Before
![before](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73faefe5-65c3-488d-a429-94edf51ca959)
After
![after](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e70f899-9353-4679-a63a-4272a8d7d197)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `/stop` command triggers abort reply with resolved prefix; normal replies still resolve correctly after model selection overwrites defaults
- Edge cases checked: No model configured (falls back to hardcoded defaults); `onModelSelected` correctly overwrites pre-seeded values at runtime

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit
- Files/config to restore: `src/channels/reply-prefix.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Incorrect model name or thinking level in reply prefix (would indicate config resolution mismatch)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `resolveDefaultModelForAgent()` or `resolveThinkingDefault()` could add overhead to context creation.
  - Mitigation: Both are lightweight synchronous config lookups with no I/O; already used elsewhere in the hot path.

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/channels/reply-prefix.ts` | Pre-seed `ResponsePrefixContext` with default model/provider/thinkingLevel from config |
| `src/channels/reply-prefix.test.ts` | New test: verifies pre-seeded context, `onModelSelected` override, and fallback defaults |


---

## Notes

- This PR is AI-assisted (fully tested: `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` — 919 test files, 2642 tests passing).
- Add before/after screenshots showing the abort reply with unresolved vs resolved template variables.
- The root cause is that the abort fast-path in `dispatch-from-config.ts` exits before the agent run triggers `onModelSelected()`, so the prefix context never got model/thinkingLevel populated. The fix is entirely in context initialization — no changes to abort or dispatch logic.

commands.author: yetval

target.title: fix(podman): mount host ~/.claude into setup container
target.body:
## Summary
- Setup wizard fails with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` under rootless Podman because `~/.claude` is never mounted into the onboard container, so `readClaudeCliCredentials` cannot find `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json`.
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the **setup container only** when `.credentials.json` exists. Gateway and other non-setup launch paths are untouched.
- Reuse the existing `ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user` + `ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user` guards on both `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR` and `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json` before mounting — rejects symlinks, wrong owner, and group/other-writable sources.
- **Soft-fail**: when guards reject the source, emit a warning and skip the mount; setup still proceeds so users configuring a non-Claude provider, or those with no `~/.claude` at all, are never blocked by Claude credential hygiene.
- Use the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the setup container exits.
- Allow overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Rootless Podman setup wizard run from a host that has a working `~/.claude/.credentials.json` fails at "Anthropic Claude CLI" onboarding with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` (#81899), because `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh` never mounted the host `.claude` directory into the setup container. After this patch the setup container gets a conditional, read-only, shared-SELinux-relabel bind mount of the host `~/.claude`, soft-gated on private-owner + symlink + permission checks, scoped to `RUN_SETUP == true` so non-setup gateway launches are unaffected, with the validation failing to a warning rather than aborting setup.

- **Real environment tested:** Fresh Fedora 43 Cloud Edition VM (DigitalOcean droplet, redacted IP), SELinux **Enforcing**, kernel `6.17.1-300.fc43.x86_64`, `podman 5.6.2`, rootless non-root `tester` user (uid 1000) with linger enabled, real `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (mode 600, owner tester), patched openclaw checkout at HEAD `5541bad4`. No mocks of the production validation helpers; only `podman` was wrapped for argv capture in the non-real-podman steps. The real-mount step uses upstream `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal` to confirm the SELinux `,z` relabel.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git fetch && git checkout 5541bad4` on the Fedora droplet as `tester`
  2. `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
  3. Positive setup with `~/.claude` perms 700/600 owned by tester — assert mount argv emitted, no warning
  4. Setup compatibility (P1 fix): chmod 640 file / chmod 770 dir / symlink file / symlink dir — assert warning printed AND setup `podman run` still invoked (exit 0, mount absent)
  5. Non-setup `launch` with loose perms — assert exit 0, gateway container starts
  6. Real container: `podman run --rm -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal /bin/bash -c "[ -r /home/node/.claude/.credentials.json ]"`
  7. Post-exit host check: `claude auth status` on the host

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live terminal output from the live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux **Enforcing** run on commit `5541bad4`. Token, email, and org UUID redacted in-place; hostname and IP redacted.

```
=== ENV (soft-fail commit 5541bad4) ===

Fedora Linux 43 (Cloud Edition) / SELinux: Enforcing / podman 5.6.2
openclaw SHA: 5541bad46f0d3354ad502d21701486005ed2519a

=== HAPPY PATH — perms 700/600 owner=tester — mount EMITTED, no warning ===

700 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude
600 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json

==podman-invoked==
argc=30
  run
  --pull=never
  --rm
  -it
  --init
  --userns=keep-id
  --user
  1000:1000
  -e
  HOME=/home/node
  -e
  TERM=xterm-256color
  -e
  BROWSER=echo
  -e
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  -e
  OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1
  --env-file
  /home/tester/.openclaw/.token.env.c3IZUX
  -v
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  -v
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
  -v
  /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z
  openclaw:local
  node
  dist/index.js
  onboard
```

```
=== STEP 3 (P1 fix) — setup + chmod 640 creds — WARN, skip mount, setup CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials file: expected owner-only permissions (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
exit=1 podman invocations (must be 1)

=== STEP 4 (P1 fix) — setup + chmod 770 dir — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials directory: group/other writable (/home/tester/.claude)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 5 (P1 fix) — setup + symlinked .credentials.json — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials file: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 6 (P1 fix) — setup + symlinked dir — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials directory: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 7 — NON-setup (gateway) + loose creds (644) — no abort, no mount, exit 0 ===

Container openclaw started: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw dashboard --no-open
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw devices approve --latest  # if pairing required
podman logs -f openclaw
For auto-start/restarts, use: ./scripts/podman/setup.sh --quadlet (Quadlet + systemd user service).

=== STEP 8 — setup happy path POST-recovery — mount re-emitted ===


=== STEP 9 — real podman ,z mount — file readable in container ===

time="2026-05-18T20:56:01Z" level=warning msg="Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root)"
time="2026-05-18T20:56:01Z" level=warning msg="Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs"
READABLE=yes

=== STEP 10 — host claude auth status post-container ===
```

- **Observed result after fix:**
  - **Setup happy path** (dir 700, file 600, owner=tester): `-v /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` emitted in the podman argv exactly once, no warning.
  - **Setup P1 compatibility fix** (unsafe credentials no longer abort setup): each of `file 640`, `dir 770`, `file is symlink`, `dir is symlink` prints `warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.` plus the exact private-check reason (`"expected owner-only permissions"`, `"group/other writable"`, `"symlinks are not allowed"`), and setup still invokes `podman run` (mock-podman shows exactly 1 invocation per case with the `.claude` mount absent). The prior review's `[P1] Preserve setup when Claude creds are unsafe — scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh:537-539` is closed: unsafe credentials no longer kill setup; non-Claude provider selection in the onboarding TUI remains reachable.
  - **Non-setup compatibility**: with loose file perms (644), symlinked `.claude`, and missing `.credentials.json` the gateway launch path completes with exit code 0; no validation abort and no `.claude` mount appears in the gateway podman argv — the prior review's `[P1] Keep Claude credential validation inside setup` stays closed.
  - **Real `podman run` with the PR's `,z` mount**: container reads `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` (`READABLE=yes`).
  - **Host `claude auth status` after container exit** reports `loggedIn: true`, confirming the host's own Claude CLI continues to authenticate (load-bearing for `,z` shared relabel vs `,Z` exclusive).

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end onboarding TUI through the production `openclaw` container image was not exercised because the proof above already covers the load-bearing wiring (script-level mount construction, soft-fail behavior, real SELinux relabel against a real `podman run`, post-exit host auth). A maintainer with a Fedora rootless Podman host can run `./scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup`, pick a provider inside the wizard, and confirm the end-to-end happy path; the diff is a small shell-script patch with no schema or migration risk.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
- [x] Live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing setup happy path — mount argv emitted
- [x] Live Fedora 43 setup with unsafe creds (P1 fix) — warning emitted, setup continues, mount absent
- [x] Live Fedora 43 non-setup gateway path with loose perms / symlink / missing creds — no abort, no mount
- [x] Real `podman run -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` — container reads credentials file
- [x] Host `claude auth status` after container exit — still logged in (proves `,z` shared relabel, not `,Z` exclusive)

## AI-assisted disclosure
- [x] AI-assisted (Claude Code, Opus 4.7).
- [x] I understand what the code does.
- [x] Human-run live proof on a real Fedora SELinux-enforcing rootless Podman host is included above.

target.title: fix(status): use latest-turn usage (not aggregate) for Context % (#83526)
target.body:
Fixes #83526.

The `📚 Context` line in TUI / `/status` output reported cumulative session token usage divided by the model's context window, producing nonsensical percentages like `2.3m/1.0m (228%)` even with `Compactions: 0` and no actual context overflow. Same root cause as #55217 (closed for Web UI) — the fix didn't propagate to the `status-message-*.js` path used by TUI and channel status cards.

Root cause path:

- `src/status/status-message.ts:284` called the **sync** entrypoint `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript`.
- That sync entrypoint routes through `extractAggregateUsageFromTranscriptChunk`, which **sums** `inputTokens` / `cacheRead` / `cacheWrite` across all tail lines.
- The prompt-tokens fallback at `status-message.ts:602` (`logUsage.promptTokens || logUsage.total`) then divided that cumulative sum by the model's context window.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts`: add `readLatestRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript` (sync) as a sibling to the existing async `readLatestRecentSessionUsageFromTranscriptAsync`. Routes through `extractLatestUsageFromTranscriptLines` so the returned snapshot reflects only the most recent transcript turn. 12-line comment block referencing #83526 and the misnamed `readLatestSessionUsageFromTranscript` sibling that confusingly aggregates.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`: re-export the new sync function.
- `src/status/status-message.ts`: switch the transcript-usage reader to the latest variant. 7-line comment block referencing #83526. Drop the aggregate-import alias (no remaining caller in this file). The aggregate function `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript` is unchanged for its other production callers (`src/gateway/session-utils.ts:703`) that legitimately want cumulative tokens.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`: 1 new regression case mirroring the existing async-latest test. Asserts a two-turn transcript yields `aggregate.inputTokens=120/outputTokens=14` and `latest.inputTokens=70/outputTokens=9`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TUI/`status` `📚 Context` showed `2.3m/1.0m (228%)` etc. with `Compactions: 0` because the cumulative cache-read total was used in the numerator instead of the current request's prompt size.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83526.mjs` builds a realistic 100-turn transcript file on disk (each turn: input=16, output=5100, cacheRead=23000, cacheWrite=110 — matching the issue's example numbers) and runs both the aggregate and latest extractors verbatim. Reproduces the 228%-class symptom exactly, then verifies the latest variant returns the sensible single-turn shape.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83526.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
=== Aggregate (current buggy behavior) ===
  inputTokens: 1616
  cacheRead:   2323000
  cacheWrite:  11110
  Context % :  234%   <-- nonsensical, issue's "228%" pattern

=== Latest (post-fix behavior) ===
  inputTokens: 16
  cacheRead:   23000
  cacheWrite:  110
  Context % :  2%   <-- sensible: current request prompt

PASS: aggregate cacheRead is 100x latest cacheRead (bug confirmed)
PASS: latest snapshot returns just-the-latest-turn cacheRead (fix working)
PASS: aggregate % (234%) exceeds 100, matching issue's "228%" symptom
PASS: latest % (2%) under 100, no spurious overflow

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The 234% aggregate value (close to the issue's 228%) is replaced by 2% with the latest-only reader. The current request's prompt size (input + cacheRead + cacheWrite of the most recent turn only) is now what the Context line shows. Other callers of the aggregate function are unchanged. The new sync function has the same signature shape as the existing async sibling, so future migration is mechanical.

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end TUI / `status` command output against a live gateway+claude-cli session. The new vitest case asserts the structural invariant against real transcript files on disk; the probe demonstrates the percentage symptom precisely.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses `extractLatestUsageFromTranscriptLines` (existing line 1424), `findExistingTranscriptPath`, `withOpenTranscriptFd`. The new sync function mirrors the existing async `readLatestRecentSessionUsageFromTranscriptAsync` in structure. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript` (aggregate) has 2 prod callers; this PR removes ONE of them (`status-message.ts:284`). The other (`session-utils.ts:703`) intentionally wants aggregate behavior. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** No open PRs on `status-message.ts`, `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript`, or this issue. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -10 -- src/status/status-message.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts` shows no recent merges. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — no external-input key copying.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(status): use latest-turn usage (not aggregate) for Context % (#83526)
target.body:
Fixes #83526.

The `📚 Context` line in TUI / `/status` output reported cumulative session token usage divided by the model's context window, producing nonsensical percentages like `2.3m/1.0m (228%)` even with `Compactions: 0` and no actual context overflow. Same root cause as #55217 (closed for Web UI) — the fix didn't propagate to the `status-message-*.js` path used by TUI and channel status cards.

Root cause path:

- `src/status/status-message.ts:284` called the **sync** entrypoint `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript`.
- That sync entrypoint routes through `extractAggregateUsageFromTranscriptChunk`, which **sums** `inputTokens` / `cacheRead` / `cacheWrite` across all tail lines.
- The prompt-tokens fallback at `status-message.ts:602` (`logUsage.promptTokens || logUsage.total`) then divided that cumulative sum by the model's context window.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts`: add `readLatestRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript` (sync) as a sibling to the existing async `readLatestRecentSessionUsageFromTranscriptAsync`. Routes through `extractLatestUsageFromTranscriptLines` so the returned snapshot reflects only the most recent transcript turn. 12-line comment block referencing #83526 and the misnamed `readLatestSessionUsageFromTranscript` sibling that confusingly aggregates.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`: re-export the new sync function.
- `src/status/status-message.ts`: switch the transcript-usage reader to the latest variant. 7-line comment block referencing #83526. Drop the aggregate-import alias (no remaining caller in this file). The aggregate function `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript` is unchanged for its other production callers (`src/gateway/session-utils.ts:703`) that legitimately want cumulative tokens.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`: 1 new regression case mirroring the existing async-latest test. Asserts a two-turn transcript yields `aggregate.inputTokens=120/outputTokens=14` and `latest.inputTokens=70/outputTokens=9`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TUI/`status` `📚 Context` showed `2.3m/1.0m (228%)` etc. with `Compactions: 0` because the cumulative cache-read total was used in the numerator instead of the current request's prompt size.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83526.mjs` builds a realistic 100-turn transcript file on disk (each turn: input=16, output=5100, cacheRead=23000, cacheWrite=110 — matching the issue's example numbers) and runs both the aggregate and latest extractors verbatim. Reproduces the 228%-class symptom exactly, then verifies the latest variant returns the sensible single-turn shape.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83526.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
=== Aggregate (current buggy behavior) ===
  inputTokens: 1616
  cacheRead:   2323000
  cacheWrite:  11110
  Context % :  234%   <-- nonsensical, issue's "228%" pattern

=== Latest (post-fix behavior) ===
  inputTokens: 16
  cacheRead:   23000
  cacheWrite:  110
  Context % :  2%   <-- sensible: current request prompt

PASS: aggregate cacheRead is 100x latest cacheRead (bug confirmed)
PASS: latest snapshot returns just-the-latest-turn cacheRead (fix working)
PASS: aggregate % (234%) exceeds 100, matching issue's "228%" symptom
PASS: latest % (2%) under 100, no spurious overflow

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The 234% aggregate value (close to the issue's 228%) is replaced by 2% with the latest-only reader. The current request's prompt size (input + cacheRead + cacheWrite of the most recent turn only) is now what the Context line shows. Other callers of the aggregate function are unchanged. The new sync function has the same signature shape as the existing async sibling, so future migration is mechanical.

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end TUI / `status` command output against a live gateway+claude-cli session. The new vitest case asserts the structural invariant against real transcript files on disk; the probe demonstrates the percentage symptom precisely.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses `extractLatestUsageFromTranscriptLines` (existing line 1424), `findExistingTranscriptPath`, `withOpenTranscriptFd`. The new sync function mirrors the existing async `readLatestRecentSessionUsageFromTranscriptAsync` in structure. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript` (aggregate) has 2 prod callers; this PR removes ONE of them (`status-message.ts:284`). The other (`session-utils.ts:703`) intentionally wants aggregate behavior. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** No open PRs on `status-message.ts`, `readRecentSessionUsageFromTranscript`, or this issue. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -10 -- src/status/status-message.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts` shows no recent merges. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — no external-input key copying.

target.title: fix(memory-core): use CJK-aware tokenizer for dreaming dedupe (#80613)
target.body:
## Summary

The dreaming-phases dedupe path's local `tokenizeSnippet` split on `/[^a-z0-9]+/i`, producing empty token sets for pure-CJK snippets and dropping all CJK content for mixed snippets. That had two failure modes on current main:

1. Two close paraphrases of the same Chinese fact tokenized to empty sets, fell back to exact-string match, returned similarity `0`, and ended up as duplicate candidates in MEMORY.md.
2. Two semantically distinct CJK snippets that happened to share ASCII tokens (e.g. `Plan` + `exRule`) returned similarity `1.0`, so the dedupe path silently dropped one of the two distinct memories.

The memory MMR layer at `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.ts` already has a CJK-aware tokenizer (unigrams + adjacent bigrams + ASCII alphanumerics). This PR extracts it into `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` and routes the dreaming dedupe path through the same helper via `textSimilarity`. `mmr.ts` re-exports `tokenize` / `jaccardSimilarity` / `textSimilarity` so existing imports (including `mmr.test.ts`) continue to work without churn.

## Root Cause

- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts:1347` (before): `function tokenizeSnippet(snippet) { return new Set(snippet.toLowerCase().split(/[^a-z0-9]+/i).map(t => t.trim()).filter(Boolean)); }`. CJK characters fall outside `[a-z0-9]`, so they are split into delimiters and dropped.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts:1357` (before): `function jaccardSimilarity(left, right)` calls the broken tokenizer and, when either token set is empty, falls back to `left.trim().toLowerCase() === right.trim().toLowerCase() ? 1 : 0`. That makes close-but-not-identical CJK pairs return 0 (missed dedup) and identical-ASCII pairs return 1 (spurious dedup, drops distinct content).
- Execution path: `dedupeEntries({ entries, threshold })` (`dreaming-phases.ts:1373`) → `jaccardSimilarity(candidate.snippet, entry.snippet) >= threshold` → either misses CJK duplicates or wrongly merges distinct CJK candidates → light dreaming output → `MEMORY.md` accumulates duplicate or loses unique entries.
- clawsweeper's review on #80613 directly endorses this fix shape: *"Keep the issue open and fix the memory-core pipeline with shared CJK-aware tokenization plus a promotion sanitizer that never appends managed dreaming block text to MEMORY.md."* and *"the narrow maintainable fix is in memory-core: reuse or extract the existing CJK-aware tokenizer for dreaming dedupe."*

## Changes

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` (NEW): extract the CJK-aware `tokenize`, `jaccardSimilarity`, and `textSimilarity` helpers from `mmr.ts` into a shared module so both consumers route through one source of truth.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.ts`: delete the in-file `CJK_RE`, `tokenize`, `jaccardSimilarity`, `textSimilarity` bodies; import them from `./tokenize.js` and re-export verbatim so existing imports (`mmr.test.ts` etc.) keep working.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts`: delete the ASCII-only `tokenizeSnippet` and local `jaccardSimilarity`. Replace the single call site in `dedupeEntries` with `snippetSimilarity` (aliased from `textSimilarity` in `./memory/tokenize.js`). Expose `dedupeEntries` via `__testing` for the regression test.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts`: add a `dedupeEntries — CJK-aware snippet similarity (#80613)` describe with 4 colocated regression cases: pure-CJK dedup, mixed-CJK kept distinct, English paraphrase unchanged, unrelated short snippets stay separate.

Net diff: **+86 / -103 LOC across 4 files** (1 new + 3 modified) — a reduction from removing the duplicate ASCII-only tokenizer.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Dreaming dedupe on CJK content is broken on current main. (1) Two close Chinese paraphrases (`教训：配置中实验开关字段是叫做规则` and `教训：配置里实验开关的字段叫做规则`) both tokenize to empty sets, fall through to exact-match, return similarity `0`, and both reach MEMORY.md instead of one merging into the other. (2) Two distinct CJK snippets that share ASCII tokens (`Plan 实验开关字段叫做 exRule` vs `Plan 整个产品体系彻底重构 exRule`) return similarity `1.0`, so the dedupe pass silently drops one of two semantically different memories. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80613
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-80613` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The before-fix smoke script reads `dreaming-phases.ts` from current `upstream/main`, extracts the production `tokenizeSnippet` + `jaccardSimilarity` bytes verbatim, and runs them against the issue's CJK scenarios. The after-fix smoke script reads the patched `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` from this PR head, prints its SHA-256, and runs the shared `tokenize` / `textSimilarity` against the same scenarios. Both scripts run via `node --experimental-strip-types` — the production function bytes drive the assertions, no mocks. PR head: `c497966ee6d12659dc750a1819969d38817f53d1`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/dreaming-cjk-tokenizer && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-verify-80613.mts`, where `smoke-verify-80613.mts` reads the patched `tokenize.ts` from `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts`, hashes it, then runs the CJK-aware `tokenize` and `textSimilarity` against the same four scenarios used in the colocated regression test (pure-CJK paraphrase, mixed-CJK distinct, English paraphrase control, unrelated short).
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `c497966ee6` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` exercised via `node --experimental-strip-types` against verbatim source bytes; tokenize.ts SHA-256 `9cd5a672bb1866c2db2384af578a8efd12d0009a69553b6d7a84cc6ee048596b`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-verify-80613.mts
=== Patched tokenize.ts SHA-256 (verbatim from worktree) ===
source bytes: 2819 (includes openclaw/plugin-sdk import line)
SHA-256 of source slice (with import line): 9cd5a672bb1866c2db2384af578a8efd12d0009a69553b6d7a84cc6ee048596b

=== Bug 2 reproduction: failure modes from issue #80613 ===
Pure-CJK A: "教训：配置中实验开关字段是叫做规则"
Pure-CJK B (similar): "教训：配置里实验开关的字段叫做规则"
tokenize(A).size = 30 first 8 tokens: [
  '教训', '配置',
  '置中', '中实',
  '实验', '验开',
  '开关', '关字'
]
tokenize(B).size = 30 first 8 tokens: [
  '教训', '配置',
  '置里', '里实',
  '实验', '验开',
  '开关', '关的'
]
textSimilarity(A, B) = 0.622 (was 0 with ASCII-only tokenizer; threshold 0.5 dedup now succeeds)

Mixed A (truth): "Plan 实验开关字段叫做 exRule"
Mixed B (unrelated): "Plan 整个产品体系彻底重构 exRule"
tokenize(A) = [
  'plan', 'exrule', '实验',
  '验开', '开关',   '关字',
  '字段', '段叫',   '叫做',
  '实',   '验',     '开',
  '关',   '字',     '段',
  '叫',   '做'
]
tokenize(B) = [
  'plan', 'exrule', '整个',
  '个产', '产品',   '品体',
  '体系', '系彻',   '彻底',
  '底重', '重构',   '整',
  '个',   '产',     '品',
  '体',   '系',     '彻',
  '底',   '重',     '构'
]
textSimilarity(A, B) = 0.056 (was 1.0 with ASCII-only tokenizer; threshold 0.7 dedup now correctly keeps both)

English A: "Plan config experiment toggle field is named exRule"
English B (paraphrase): "Plan configuration uses experiment toggle field named exRule"
textSimilarity(en1, en2) = 0.600 (must stay > 0.4 — Latin-script behavior unchanged)

textSimilarity('weather: sunny', 'deploy: blocked') = 0.000 (must stay < 0.3 — no over-collapse)

=== Pass/fail summary ===
[CJK paraphrase dedups]  textSimilarity = 0.622 PASS (was 0, dedup now succeeds)
[Mixed CJK kept distinct] textSimilarity = 0.056 PASS (was 1.0, two distinct facts now kept)
[English paraphrase OK]   textSimilarity = 0.600 PASS (Latin behavior unchanged)
[Unrelated short stays]   textSimilarity = 0.000 PASS (no over-collapse)
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: Pure-CJK paraphrase similarity went from `0.000` (was missed by ASCII-only tokenizer falling through to exact-match) to **0.622** — above the typical `0.5–0.6` dedupe threshold, so the second copy is now correctly merged. The mixed-CJK distinct pair went from `1.000` (spurious merge that silently dropped one of two distinct memories) to **0.056** — well below the `0.7` threshold, so both semantically different snippets are kept. The English paraphrase control stayed at `0.600` (Latin-script behavior unchanged). Unrelated short snippets stayed at `0.000` (no over-collapse). The patched `tokenize.ts` is pinned by SHA-256 `9cd5a672bb1866c2db2384af578a8efd12d0009a69553b6d7a84cc6ee048596b`, so the proof is bound to the exact source bytes this PR ships.
- **What was not tested**: A full end-to-end light-dreaming sweep against a real CJK workspace was not run on the contributor machine (would need a populated `~/.openclaw/workspace` with Chinese daily memory files and a configured agent). The fix is a pure-function tokenizer change at the `dedupeEntries` boundary; the downstream snippet-promotion path (`short-term-promotion.ts`) consumes the returned `ShortTermRecallEntry[]` verbatim, so the only behavioral change is the now-correct dedupe arithmetic. Bug 1 from the same issue (managed dreaming block content leaking into promoted MEMORY.md entries via `buildDailySnippetChunks`/`stripManagedDailyDreamingLines` boundary mismatch) is **intentionally out of scope** here — clawsweeper flagged it as not high-confidence-reproducible from source alone (`"the raw managed-block leak has a credible source path through promotion rehydration, but still needs a focused regression to pin the exact current-main fixture"`), and the right shape is a separate promotion-path PR with a targeted fixture. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live CJK workspace recording is required.

## Test

- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — new `dedupeEntries — CJK-aware snippet similarity (#80613)` describe with 4 cases (CJK paraphrase dedup / mixed-CJK kept distinct / English paraphrase control / unrelated short kept separate); all existing cases continue to pass.
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.test.ts` — unchanged; the re-exports of `tokenize` / `jaccardSimilarity` / `textSimilarity` from `mmr.ts` preserve the existing import surface so this test stays green.
- `pnpm tsgo` against the touched files — clean (LSP diagnostics on `tokenize.ts`, `mmr.ts`, `dreaming-phases.ts`, `dreaming-phases.test.ts` show only pre-existing environment-only `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` resolution warnings; no new TypeScript errors introduced).

## Notes

- Scope: Bug 2 from issue #80613 only (the CJK dedupe path that clawsweeper marked as source-reproducible). Bug 1 (managed-block leak into MEMORY.md) is a separate fixture problem that clawsweeper said "still needs a focused regression to pin the exact current-main fixture" — it should land in a separate promotion-path PR.
- Compatibility: `mmr.ts` re-exports `tokenize` / `jaccardSimilarity` / `textSimilarity` so every existing call site (including `mmr.test.ts`) keeps working without an import-path change. The CJK-aware tokenizer is byte-for-byte the same algorithm `mmr.ts` already shipped, so MMR behavior is unchanged.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #80613: *"the narrow maintainable fix is in memory-core: reuse or extract the existing CJK-aware tokenizer for dreaming dedupe... rather than adding a new config or policy knob."*

Closes #80613

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
target.author: alaleiwang
